title: Col Serra
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Serra}}

}}
{{Quote|I see Col Serra. He's one of Han Solo's buddies. Get ready for a tough fight.|Storm commando captain Sarkli|<ref name="Threat of the Conqueror DS">{{SWGTCG|set=Threat of the Conqueror|scenario=Dark Side scenario campaign}}</ref>}}
'''Col Serra''', a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] who operated during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and was an old friend of another smuggler, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Serra was asked by Solo, who had joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], to assemble a new unit of trained [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] for the Rebellion. Serra scoured [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], recruiting a ragtag bunch of [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrels]], [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], and other outlaws, forming [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]] in the process. Serra himself became the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the new unit. The Renegades first saw action during the [[Evacuation of Yavin|evacuation]] of the [[Great Temple/Legends|Rebel base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], where they acted as a cover for the retreating Rebel [[Troop/Legends|troops]], who fled from the invading forces of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. During the battle, Serra was assisted by the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] [[X2]], and the two of them managed to take down an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] by destroying its [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]]. Following the evacuation, Serra and Renegade [[Squadron/Legends|Squadron]] helped Commander [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] to find a suitable location for a [[Boz Pity base|new Rebel base]]. Once it was established on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Boz Pity/Legends|Boz Pity]], though, the Rebels had to escape again, as the Empire had [[Battle of Boz Pity (Galactic Civil War)/Legends|followed]] them to the planet.

When the Alliance had finally settled on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], Serra and Renegade Squadron began carrying out constant strikes against the Empire. They [[Mission to Rori|attacked]] an Imperial [[Rori starship repair facility|starship repair facility]] on [[Rori/Legends|Rori]], and an Imperial [[Naboo weapons depot|weapons depot]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. During the latter [[Mission to Naboo (Renegade Squadron)|mission]], Serra and his men uncovered evidence about a new Imperial [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], the [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]]-equipped [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Conqueror (superlaser)|''Conqueror'']]. Serra and Renegade Squadron undertook a [[Mission to Mustafar (Renegade Squadron)|daring mission]] to the [[Mustafar system/Legends|Mustafar system]] to destroy the ''Conqueror'' and successfully carried out their objective despite the intervention of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] himself. When the Empire finally discovered [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on Hoth in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Serra and the Renegades once again assumed the role of a rearguard for the Rebel [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|evacuation]], planting explosives throughout the base and escaping in the last moment before its destruction.

[[4 ABY/Legends|One year later]], Serra's squadron played a crucial role in both the run-up to the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and the battle itself, during which they assisted Solo's [[Endor strike team/Legends|strike team]] in destroying the [[Endor shield generator bunker|shield generator bunker]] that projected the [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] protecting the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Imperial Death Star]]. On [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], Serra once again teamed up with X2, and together they helped Rebel ground and [[Realspace/Legends|space]] forces, sabotaging the Imperial communications network and taking down shields of enemy Star [[Destroyer/Legends|Destroyers]]. After the battle was won by the Alliance, Serra and his men disbanded Renegade Squadron, returning to their underworld activities. Many [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, Serra was located by the [[Jedi archivist/Legends|Jedi archivist]] [[Tionne Solusar|Tionne]], who was compiling a record on Renegade Squadron. Serra then told her about his and his squadron's role in the Rebellion's fight against the Empire.

==Biography==
===From smuggler to Rebel===
{{Quote|Smugglers, pirates, gunfighters—it didn't matter what they had been before. All that mattered was that they were willing to fight—and maybe die—for the Alliance. I tracked them down and offered them whatever it took. In the end, I found enough of what I was looking for.|Col Serra, on the Renegade Squadron members|<ref name="RS" />}}
[[File:Serra_facing_Renegade_Squadron.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Col Serra facing the new recruits]]
Col Serra was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] active during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], who was friends with fellow smuggler [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. At some point in his life, Serra lost his left [[Eye/Legends|eye]] and replaced it with a [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic patch]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Solo—now allied with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]—asked Serra to recruit [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] that did not need any additional training in order to strengthen the defenses of the [[Great Temple/Legends|Alliance base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], which was soon going to be attacked by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in retaliation for the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] by the Rebels. At the time, the Alliance was mostly made up of [[Farm/Legends|farmboys]] and dreamers, and the Rebellion needed a force of formidable fighters<ref name="RS" /> who knew military tactics and could follow orders without question. Serra accepted the mission and began searching [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for the right mix of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], and other renegades who would agree to fight for the Alliance.<ref name="Blog: Col Serra">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ign.com/blogs/renegadesquadron/2007/08/07/col-serra-who-is-this-guy|text=Col Serra: Who Is This Guy?|date=2007-08-07|archiveurl=https://archive.is/CQtL2}}</ref> It turned out to be somewhat easy work, due to the abundance of individuals in the galaxy who hated the Empire.<ref name="RS" /> Unexpectedly for Serra, many of the recruits even brought their own stolen [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] for the cause.<ref name="Renegade Pilot card">{{SWGTCG|set=Threat of the Conqueror|cardname=Renegade Pilot}}</ref>

Soon enough, Serra's task was complete, and he found the right amount of soldiers. Most of them were wanted men who had been on the run from [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunters and were used to keeping a low profile, so they made sure that there were no records of them in the Alliance archives. Serra then became the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]]—as the newly formed unit had been christened—and together with Solo, started to prepare them to fight against the Empire. Soon after the formation of the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]], the Yavin base was invaded, forcing the Alliance to begin a full-scale [[Evacuation of Yavin|evacuation]]. Renegade Squadron acted as a cover for the retreating forces and pushed the invading enemy back from the Alliance's main base temple, but one Imperial [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout trooper]] infiltrated the temple and stole the Alliance communication codes. Renegade Squadron hunted down the [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] and returned the logs back to the temple. After destroying all databanks with sensitive information to prevent it from falling into enemy hands,<ref name="RS" /> the Renegades and Serra boarded their [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] starfighters and joined the battle in [[Realspace/Legends|space]], where they engaged an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]].<ref name="ES">''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron]]'' {{C|[[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PSP]] version}}</ref>

The squadron could not handle the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] by themselves, though, so Serra called in [[X2]], a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] and the [[Grey Leader|leader]] of [[Grey Squadron]], and asked him for assistance. X2, who was still on the ground when Serra contacted him, soon joined the space battle in his X-wing. Serra told him to help Renegade Squadron take down the Star [[Destroyer/Legends|Destroyer's]] communications array, to prevent the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] from calling reinforcements. Once the array was destroyed, X2 and Serra landed their X-wings inside the Star Destroyer's [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] and fought their way through [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to the ship's [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]]. X2 destroyed the core and the two returned to their starfighters, successfully evacuating just as the Star Destroyer exploded, thus buying the necessary [[Time/Legends|time]] for the Alliance to complete the evacuation.<ref name="ES" />

===On the run===
{{Quote|Solo might not shoot first when it comes to bounty hunters…me and the rest of the squadron, though, we had our own views on that.|Serra|<ref name="RS" />}}
After the retreat from the Yavin base, the Alliance was faced with a difficult challenge of finding a new central base to operate from. It was soon discovered, however, that before the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] had been [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] by the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], the Rebel leader [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]] had encrypted a list of possible locations for a base into a [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] encased in [[Phrik/Legends|phrik]], a material strong enough to have survived the planet's destruction. To this end, an [[Battle of the Graveyard|operation]] was organized to recover the holocron, with Solo given command of a Rebel [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] consisting of a [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]], two [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigates]] and a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|Corellian corvette]]. Renegade Squadron [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] also participated in the attack and were stationed along with their starfighters aboard the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Cruiser/Legends|Cruiser]]. When Solo's fleet had reached [[The Graveyard|the remains of Alderaan]], however, they discovered an Imperial force there. Despite the presence of the Imperials and the sudden intervention of the bounty hunter [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], the Renegades were able to retrieve the holocron, and Serra transmitted its contents to the Rebel Commander [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], who was tasked with finding a new base.<ref name="RS" />

[[File:OrdMantellCantina.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Serra, Solo, and members of Renegade Squadron in a cantina on Ord Mantell]]
Serra was then ordered to go to [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] to rendezvous with Solo, where the latter was in the middle of repairs to his starship, the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], after the damage it had sustained during the battle at Alderaan's remains. Ord Mantell was a backwater world with only a small Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]], so Serra and Renegade Squadron believed that they would be safe there and decided to have some rest. When Serra, Solo, and the Renegades were relaxing in a local [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], however, bounty hunter [[IG-88B|IG-88]] burst in, intent on collecting the bounty on them. A [[Skirmish on Ord Mantell (0.5 ABY)|firefight]] ensued, and soon enough the entire Imperial garrison was after Serra and Renegade Squadron. The Renegades needed to get off-planet quickly before Imperial reinforcements arrived. Nevertheless, the ''Falcon'' still needed some repairs, so the Renegades, along with Solo's co-pilot [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], had to scour the nearby junkyard for spare parts. After finishing the repairs, defeating IG-88 in a shootout, and destroying an [[Anti-aircraft gun|anti-aircraft turret]], Serra and Renegade Squadron evacuated on the ''Falcon'', just as Imperial Star Destroyers appeared above the planet.<ref name="RS" />

Still shaken after their disastrous vacation, Serra and Solo received more bad news from [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance Command]]. While checking the sites listed in the holocron, Commander Ackbar had been captured by the Empire and was being transported to the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] world of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. Many of Serra's friends from his smuggling days had been sent there, never to return again, so he was determined not to let the same happen to Ackbar, the Alliance's greatest tactician. A rescue operation was quickly set in motion, and the Rebel fleet [[Battle of Kessel (0 ABY)|attacked]] the Star Destroyer that was transporting Ackbar over Kessel. During the battle, Renegade Squadron used an [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/Legends|LAAT]] to land in the Star Destroyer's hangar. They located Ackbar inside, escorted him to the LAAT, and flew back to the Rebel Mon Calamari Cruiser. Once in safety, Ackbar told his rescuers the story of his capture. He had been investigating the world of [[Boz Pity/Legends|Boz Pity]] and had found it to be an ideal place for a new base. Deciding to visit other worlds on Organa's list, he took a group of [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] and [[Droid/Legends|droids]] to the planet of [[Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami]]. They never reached their destination, though, as their [[Transport/Legends|transport]] was [[Skirmish over Saleucami|attacked]] by Boba Fett, and those aboard were captured by the bounty hunter. Fett sold Ackbar to the Imperials and took the rest of Ackbar's crew to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] to sell them to the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]].<ref name="RS" />

Not willing to abandon the scout team, Serra, Solo, and Renegade Squadron set off to Tatooine to [[Mission to Mos Eisley (Galactic Civil War)|rescue]] them. After landing in the planet's [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], the Renegades, on Serra's advice, dispatched the Imperials stationed in the local [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Cantina]]. Inside the cantina, they found a [[Unidentified Rodian contact|Rodian]], who told them that the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] were being held in a [[Mos Eisley shipyard|nearby shipyard]] and promised to show the way. Serra was initially distrustful as to the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian's]] information, but he agreed to follow him, as the Rodian was Renegade Squadron's only hope to find the prisoners. The squadron soon reached the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]], but found it sealed by a force field. After destroying [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|force field generators]] scattered throughout Mos Eisley and clearing the shipyard of enemy [[Troop/Legends|troops]], the Renegades found the prisoners kept in a small sealed cell inside the shipyard. Solo used his [[Fusioncutter/Legends|fusioncutter]] to break them out, and after destroying two [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]], Renegade Squadron and the rescued prisoners evacuated on the ''Falcon''.<ref name="RS" />

[[File:BattleOfBozPity-SWBFRS.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The battle of Boz Pity rages on.]]
Rejoining with the rest of the Alliance forces, Serra and Renegade Squadron traveled to Boz Pity, where the Alliance established a [[Boz Pity base|new base]]. Only a few [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] after that, however, an Imperial assault force [[Battle of Boz Pity (Galactic Civil War)/Legends|attacked]] the base. It turned out that Fett had placed a [[Homing beacon/Legends|tracking beacon]] onto an [[R5-series astromech droid|R5 droid]] that Renegade Squadron had rescued from Tatooine. The Rebels had to fight against a sizable Imperial force, complete with [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] walkers, but managed to stall the enemy thanks to a clever usage of [[particle cannon turret]]s and booby traps. While Renegade Squadron held their line, Solo and Chewbacca prepared the evacuation craft for takeoff. In the end, the Alliance was able to safely evacuate from Boz Pity. Eventually, Rebel [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]-in-[[Jedi training/Legends|training]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] discovered the remote [[Water/Legends|ice]] planet of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], which became the site of [[Echo Base/Legends|the new Rebel base]] for the next few [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. Renegade Squadron used Hoth as their base of operations, continuously undertaking missions against the Empire,<ref name="RS" /> and forcing [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to assign his elite [[Storm commando/Legends|storm commandos]] to hunt down the Renegades.<ref name="Threat of the Conqueror DS" />

===Later missions===
{{Quote|It was Skywalker that discovered Hoth, an ice world so remote it didn't appear on any of the regular charts. For a few cold years it was our home, a place to strike back against the Empire. If the Imperials hadn't heard of Renegade Squadron before, they knew it now. We hit them hard and fast wherever we found them, vanishing before they barely knew we'd been there. It felt good to know that we didn't have to run anymore.|Serra|<ref name="RS" />}}
In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], a shipment of [[Ryll/Legends|ryll]] headed for a Rebel medical facility went missing, and the Rebels dispatched several agents to find it. Some time later, Solo was assigned to go to [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] to meet with one of the agents, [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Coret Bhan]]. Serra and a group of Rebels accompanied Solo to Corellia. While Serra and Solo were waiting for Bhan in a local cantina, the rest of the Rebels took position outside. Instead of Bhan, however, a mysterious smuggler soon appeared in the cantina. Offering Solo a drink, he started questioning him about the ryll shipment. Somehow, Serra discovered that the smuggler was in fact an agent of the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] who was also tracking the shipment and who had subdued Bhan on Tatooine. Serra approached Solo and warned his friend about the agent, while at the same time the remaining Rebels entered the cantina, their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] drawn. A fight broke out, and the Imperial agent emerged victorious. He subdued Solo and the Rebels, but let them go immediately afterward. Unbeknownst to Solo, however, the agent had planted a homing beacon on him during the cantina fight, so when the shipment was finally located in the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]] [[Smuggler's Run/Legends|Smuggler's Run]], and Solo set off to retrieve it, the agent followed him and ambushed him while Solo, Chewbacca, and Solo's friend [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] were loading the cargo into the ''Millennium Falcon''. Solo managed to get away with only a few crates of ryll, while the rest of the cargo was claimed by the agent in favor of the Empire.<ref name="Agents of Deception">{{SWGTCG|set=Agents of Deception|scenario=Dark Side scenario campaign}}</ref>

[[File:Col_Serra_TOTC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Serra in action]]
Some time later, Serra led Renegade Squadron in an [[Mission to Rori|attack]] on an Imperial [[Rori starship repair facility|starship repair facility]] on [[Rori/Legends|Rori]]. Landing in an [[Assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttle]], the Renegades assaulted the facility, expecting to easily overwhelm the garrisoned troops. Serra and his men, however, were surprised to find a unit of storm [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] there, who were repairing their own starship at the time. The storm commandos put up a fierce resistance, forcing Serra and Renegade Squadron to fall back to their assault [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] and retreat from the planet. Fortunately for Serra, the storm commandos' ship had not been repaired yet, so they could not pursue Renegade Squadron. With the Renegades off-planet, however, a [[Zephyr Base|secret Rebel outpost]] on Rori was left virtually defenseless. The storm commandos attacked it and found some valuable intel there concerning Rebel sympathizers among the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]].<ref name="Threat of the Conqueror DS" />

Renegade Squadron's next [[Mission to Naboo (Renegade Squadron)|mission]] was to take out an Imperial [[Naboo weapons depot|weapons depot]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. Before that, Serra personally contacted and recruited into his squadron a Rebel who had already managed to gain some reputation in the fight against the Empire. Once he joined the team, Renegade Squadron embarked on the mission. Reaching position near the depot, Serra and the new Renegade took a look around with [[Electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]], only to find the place guarded by storm commandos led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Sarkli]], coincidentally the same commandos that Renegade Squadron had fought on Rori. Fully realizing the threat the storm commandos posed, Serra still decided to attack the depot. The Renegades managed to make it halfway to the depot building before the storm commandos spotted them, sounded an alarm, and started firing from the roof of the building. Serra and his men took cover and engaged Sarkli's troops, ultimately forcing them to escape into the wilderness.<ref name="Threat of the Conqueror LS">{{SWGTCG|set=Threat of the Conqueror|scenario=Light Side scenario campaign}}</ref>

Inside the depot, the Renegades found a number of crates with weapons and ammunition and one crate filled with [[Datafile/Legends|datafiles]], which they decided to check. One of the datafiles showed an image of an ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer. Serra found nothing special with that, but the new recruit noticed a strange weapon at the Star Destroyer's bow. Serra then decided to collect all the files from the depot and let Rebel [[Slicer/Legends|slicers]] take a look at them. Analyzing the data from the depot, the slicers soon found out that the Star Destroyer was equipped with a powerful [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]], much like the one on the Death Star. The files also showed that the superlaser had been developed in a [[Imperial Weapon Research Facility|research facility]] on Naboo, and Serra picked it as the Renegades' next target, hoping to learn more about the Star Destroyer. Infiltrating the facility, Renegade Squadron proceeded to look for the main control room, but were soon confronted by [[Sith acolyte/Legends|Sith Acolyte]] [[Namman Cha]], who was determined to keep the facility's secrets safe and attacked the Renegades. Cha fought ferociously, but Serra's men managed to overwhelm him with their superior numbers, and Cha retreated. Renegade Squadron then returned to their objective and soon found the main control room. The slicers downloaded all the data kept there, which revealed the Star Destroyer's name, the [[Conqueror (superlaser)|''Conqueror'']], as well as its location—the [[Mustafar system/Legends|Mustafar system]].<ref name="Threat of the Conqueror LS" />

===One less superweapon===
{{Quote|Admiral Strang? Victor Strang? He's the captain? He used to lead a team of Storm Commandos. Stay sharp, men. This will be no ordinary boarding action.|Serra, to Renegade Squadron, prior to boarding the ''Conqueror''|<ref name="Renegade Pilot card" />}}
The data also showcased the ''Conqueror'' firing at an [[Unidentified Mustafar system planetoid|uninhabited planetoid]] in the [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] [[Star system/Legends|system]] as a test for the superlaser, obliterating the planet completely. Serra was shocked by the starship's firepower, and Renegade Squadron set off to Mustafar to [[Mission to Mustafar (Renegade Squadron)|destroy]] the vessel. There, the Renegades met with a Rebel [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|intelligence]] [[Unidentified Alliance Intelligence officer|officer]], who told them that supply shuttles from the ''Conqueror'' landed on Mustafar once a day. With this information, Serra and Renegade Squadron decided to use a shuttle to infiltrate the ''Conqueror''. They went to the shuttles' landing site and lay in wait. They soon witnessed the [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]] arriving at the site with a group of [[Blackguard (Mustafar)/Legends|Blackguard]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]. Serra realized that the fact that [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] and the Blackguard were working together meant that the ''Conqueror'' project was very important to the Empire. Deciding to take Isard down before the shuttle arrived, Renegade Squadron attacked her. After a brief shootout, Isard retreated along with the Blackguard.<ref name="Threat of the Conqueror LS" />

[[File:Conqueror_superlaser.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The ''Conqueror'', Renegade Squadron's target]]
Serra and Renegade Squadron then returned to their cover. When the shuttle arrived, the Renegades easily subdued its crew and put on their [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|uniforms]]. Aboard the shuttle, Serra discovered that the ''Conqueror'' was captained by [[Victor Strang]], a former storm commando. Having heard about Strang, Serra advised his men to prepare for heavy resistance. The shuttle soon landed in the ''Conqueror''{{'s}} landing bay. Renegade Squadron's Imperial uniforms provided the necessary distraction, and Serra's men were able to quickly dispatch the landing bay's guards. Shedding the stolen uniforms, Serra and Renegade Squadron proceeded to the ship's reactor core, but were stopped just before it by a set of [[Blast door/Legends|blast doors]]. On Serra's command, his troopers planted some explosives on the doors, blowing them open, only to find Strang and a team of storm commandos waiting for them on the other side, already warned of Renegade Squadron's mission by Isard. The ensuing battle was fierce, but swift. Renegade Squadron used overlapping support fire, gaining an advantage and forcing Strang to retreat.<ref name="Threat of the Conqueror LS" />

Before the Renegades could pursue him, another blast door slammed shut behind Strang's back, and Serra commanded his troops to proceed with their original mission. Inside the reactor core room, Renegade Squadron's demolition experts set explosive charges on the reactor core, and the squadron made their way back to the landing bay. As they prepared to enter the shuttle that had brought them aboard, they were surprised to see the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] emerging from it, escorted by a squad of elite stormtroopers. Vader was determined to ensure that the ''Conqueror'' would be delivered safely to the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. Another battle began, and when Vader's men took cover behind one of the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttles]] that were stationed in the landing bay, the Renegades fired [[Missile/Legends|missiles]] at it. The shuttle was destroyed, and Vader and his stormtroopers were temporarily stalled by the explosion. Serra and the Renegades were quick to use this pause to their advantage, boarding the supply shuttle and blasting off from the ''Conqueror''. Explosions started to tear the Star Destroyer apart, but a [[TIE Advanced x1/Legends|TIE Advanced]] and an [[X4 Gunship/Legends|Incom gunship]] also managed to evacuate before there was nothing left of the ''Conqueror''. Serra believed that Vader had been [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the explosion, but the new Renegade correctly guessed that the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] Advanced had been piloted by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. The trooper also believed that Strang might have escaped on the [[Gunship/Legends|gunboat]]. Still, the Renegades' mission was a success, and the ''Conqueror'' was no longer a threat to the Alliance.<ref name="Threat of the Conqueror LS" />

===A bitter winter===
{{Quote|Everybody has heard of the Empire's victory at Hoth that day. What no one knows, though, is that without Renegade Squadron, ''nobody'' would have made it off Hoth alive.|Col Serra, on Renegade Squadron's role in the Battle of Hoth|<ref name="RS" />}}
In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the Empire discovered the Rebels' Echo Base on Hoth and launched a [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|full-scale invasion]]. Renegade Squadron was once again chosen to act as a cover for the retreating Rebel troops. Having destroyed the base's shield generator, the Imperial forces entered the base. Receiving orders from [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]], Serra directed his unit to the base's control room to destroy databanks with sensitive information to prevent it from falling to the Imperials. Meanwhile, Serra himself planted explosive charges throughout Echo Base. After Renegade Squadron destroyed the databanks, they set off timers on the explosives planted by Serra. The Renegades were then ordered to protect the crew of the Rebel [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]], which was used to clear the way for the retreating [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|Rebel transports]]. Once the ion cannon crew evacuated, Serra and the Renegades proceeded to the extraction point as well, being the last squadron to evacuate from Hoth as the explosives detonated, destroying Echo Base and leaving the Empire with nothing but dust on their hands.<ref name="RS" />

[[File:Col_Serra_headshot.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Commander Col Serra]]
While the ground battle came to a close, the fight in space above Hoth still continued, where the Rebel fleet consisting of a Mon Calamari cruiser and Nebulon-B [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]] protected the last transport that had not evacuated yet. The Renegades escorted the transport, helping it to safely make a jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Serra's squadron engaged the Empire's experimental [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]] as the rest of the fleet were preparing to make a hyperspace jump. Serra then ordered the Renegades to commandeer one of the TIE Defenders to study it. The Renegades completed their mission, and after defeating Darth Vader himself in his TIE Advanced, they fled from Hoth and regrouped with the rest of the Alliance forces. The defeat at Hoth, combined with the loss of Solo, who had been [[Occupation of Bespin|encased]] in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] and claimed by Fett, badly influenced the Rebels' morale. Nevertheless, the Renegades continued their service, participating in numerous battles and trying to show with their actions that there was still some hope for the Rebellion.<ref name="RS" />

A [[4 ABY/Legends|year after the Battle of Hoth]], Solo was finally [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon|rescued]] by Skywalker and his friends. Additionally, it was discovered that the Empire was building a [[Death Star II/Legends|new Death Star]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the [[Endor/Legends|forest moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]]. The Death Star, however, was protected by a defensive [[Deflector shield/Legends|energy shield]] projected from the surface of the planet. Solo, now promoted to general, decided to lead a [[Endor strike team/Legends|strike team]] to the surface of the planet by using the stolen Imperial shuttle [[Tydirium/Legends|''Tydirium'']] and take down the shield, allowing the Rebel fleet to destroy the Death Star. Before proceeding with the mission, though, the Rebels needed to learn more about the Imperial positions on Endor. When it was discovered that the Emperor was going to make a pilgrimage to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] tomb world of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], a [[Battle of Korriban (Galactic Civil War)|mission]] was organized to learn more about Endor's defenses from his personal shuttle. Every member of Renegade Squadron volunteered for the mission, which was described by Serra as "crazy" and "foolhardy."<ref name="RS" />

The Renegades arrived on Korriban before Palpatine did and captured the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] and a Sith tomb. Serra then placed explosive charges on all three exits from the tomb, hoping to collapse them and trap Palpatine inside once he entered it. To lure the Emperor inside, the Renegades destroyed some [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] in the tomb. Enraged, Palpatine entered the tomb and was trapped there when the exits collapsed, allowing the Renegades to retrieve the data from his shuttle. The data showed that Palpatine was expected to soon visit the Death Star with an inspection. The Alliance decided to take their chances and destroy both the second Death Star and the Emperor with [[Battle of Endor/Legends|one single blow]]. To lure at least some of the Imperials away from Endor, a [[Battle of Sullust (4 ABY)|diversionary assault]] on the Imperial-controlled planet of [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] was organized, and Renegade Squadron was assigned to carry out the mission.<ref name="RS" />

The Renegades were disappointed that they would not be present on Endor, but agreed to participate in the Sullust raid, realizing its importance. Arriving on the planet, Renegade Squadron captured the Imperial garrison building without any resistance, leading Serra to believe that something was not right. He then decided to contact Alliance Command to tell them about his thoughts. Serra's suspicions proved true—the Renegades had walked into a trap on Sullust. Soon, IG-88 appeared, who, as Serra suspected, had been hired by Vader because of the problems Renegade Squadron had caused to the latter on Hoth. Moreover, the Empire was using [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] to keep the Renegades stranded on Sullust. Renegade Squadron was able to defeat IG-88 in a fight once again, and they destroyed the generators, eventually securing Sullust.<ref name="RS" />

===The last battle===
{{Quote|This is one victory that'll go down in history for sure.|Serra, during the Battle of Endor|<ref name="ES" />}}
Serra's men then immediately traveled to Endor, arriving just in time to slip past the shield through an opening created for Solo's strike team. Renegade Squadron landed some way from Solo, and soon discovered that Solo had been pinned down near the [[Endor shield generator bunker|shield generator bunker]] by the Imperial troops. To make matters worse, the entire Death Star affair was revealed to be a trap set by Palpatine, as the Death Star turned out to be operational and the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial fleet]] emerged at Endor to battle the Rebels. As the full-scale battle began, Renegade Squadron fought their way through Imperial troops on the surface, hoping to reach Solo's team and help him destroy the bunker.<ref name="RS" /> Serra himself opted to fly his X-wing to give his men air support. Serra then contacted X2, who also participated in the battle and was at the time defending [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Ackbar's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']], from invading enemy forces. Serra requested that X2 assist Renegade Squadron on the surface. After securing ''Home One'', X2 used an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] to reach Endor's surface. Serra then contacted X2 once again, telling him that several of the native [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] who had been helping Solo's team and Renegade Squadron had been captured by the Empire. After X2 rescued them, Serra decided to join him on the ground.<ref name="ES" />

When he began his landing run, however, his X-wing was shot down by a [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] battery, and Serra crashed the starfighter into a [[Tree/Legends|tree]]. He survived the crash and rendezvoused with X2, and together, they made their way into the bunker. They sabotaged the Imperial communications network, leaving Solo to destroy the bunker itself.<ref name="ES" /> Soon enough, Solo's strike team and Renegade Squadron destroyed the bunker, lowering the Death Star's shield.<ref name="RS" /> This created an opportunity for Lando Calrissian—now also a Rebel general—to fly the ''Millennium Falcon'' into the Death Star's inner structure and destroy its reactor core, causing a chain reaction that destroyed the superweapon. Prior to the Death Star's destruction, Skywalker had turned Darth Vader to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]], and Vader had killed Palpatine, though he had sacrificed his life in the process.<ref name="Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi">{{Film|VI}}</ref> Nevertheless, both the battle in space and on the ground continued. While Renegade Squadron mopped up the remnants of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]], Serra and X2 made their way to an Imperial ion cannon, using it to disable the shields of two Star Destroyers in orbit, leaving them open for Ackbar's fleet to destroy.<ref name="ES" />

Meanwhile, two AT-ATs attacked the nearby Ewok village, and Serra told X2 to destroy them. X2 used a [[Missile launcher/Legends|rocket launcher]], targeting the AT-ATs' cranial section, ultimately taking down the vehicles.<ref name="ES" /> Renegade Squadron managed to deal with the rest of the enemy troops, and the battle was won by the Rebels. Following the Battle of Endor, Renegade Squadron decided to disband themselves, believing that the upstart [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] needed heroes like Skywalker, Solo, and Calrissian, and not the ragtag bunch that was the Renegades. After disbanding the unit, its members, including Serra, returned to a life of crime.

===Post-war===
[[File:Tionne_and_Serra.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Serra conversing with Tionne via a hologram]]
Many years later, Serra was contacted by the [[Jedi archivist/Legends|archivist]] of the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Tionne Solusar|Tionne]], via a [[Hologram/Legends|hololink]]. She had found old, encrypted data about Renegade Squadron, but it did not reveal much. With much effort, she located Serra and asked him about Renegade Squadron. Believing that enough time had passed since the end of the Rebellion and that people deserved to know about Renegade Squadron, he went on to tell her about the squadron's exploits.<ref name="RS" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I must say, commander. You are a most difficult man to track down.''"<br />"''I've found I generally live longer that way.|Tionne and Serra, upon meeting for the first time|<ref name="RS" />}}
[[File:Col_Serra_SWGTCG-AoD.jpg|thumb|right|Col Serra sitting in a cantina]]
Gruff and battle-weary, Serra was nevertheless a noble person.<ref name="Blog: Creating a new character">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ign.com/blogs/renegadesquadron/2007/09/07/creating-a-new-star-wars-character|text=Creating a New Star Wars Character|date=2007-08-28|archiveurl=https://archive.is/BNOLl}}</ref> He had a very friendly relationship with Han Solo, responding to his request to form a new squadron without hesitation. He was cautious and attentive to details, not trusting the Rodian's information on Tatooine and suspecting a trap on Sullust after Renegade Squadron captured the garrison building too easily.<ref name="RS" /> While viewing the datafiles on Naboo, though, he failed to notice the strange weapon installed on the Star Destroyer's bow. He later believed that Renegade Squadron had killed Vader, not suspecting that the latter could have escaped on the TIE Advanced.<ref name="Threat of the Conqueror LS" /> Serra hated being constantly on the run from the Empire after the evacuation of Yavin and was relieved when the Rebel Alliance finally settled on Hoth and started striking back at the Empire. After the defeat at Hoth, Serra and his squadron tried their best to keep the Rebels' hopes up.<ref name="RS" />

Unlike Solo, who preferred not to shoot first when dealing with bounty hunters, Serra demonstrated the exact opposite attitude. Having survived many battles alongside Renegade Squadron, he came to trust his men completely and was willing to even entrust them with his life.<ref name="RS" /> In turn, Renegade Squadron and Serra's reputation was such that the elite storm commandos considered them a threat to be reckoned with.<ref name="Threat of the Conqueror DS" /> As a smuggler, Serra preferred to keep a low profile and did not talk about his shady business over an open hololink, believing that such an attitude had helped him to stay alive for so long. Serra had light skin and [[Color/Legends|brown]] eyes. His brown [[Hair/Legends|hair]] had started graying by the time of the Renegade Squadron's creation.<ref name="RS" />

==Behind the scenes==
Col Serra was created for the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]'', developed by [[Rebellion Developments]] and released in [[2007]], exclusively on the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PlayStation Portable]]. In the game, he served as the narrator of the campaign mode.<ref name="RS" /> In [[June]] of [[2009]], Serra was briefly featured in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] scenario campaign of ''[[Agents of Deception]]'', an expansion set of the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game]]''. A card depicting Serra was also included in the set, though it mistakenly claimed that he commanded [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] instead of Renegade Squadron. The mistake was later corrected in a subsequent patch.<ref name="Agents card">{{SWGTCG|set=Agents of Deception|cardname=Col Serra}}</ref> In [[November]] of the same year, Serra was featured in the PSP version of another ''Battlefront'' game, ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron]]''. This time, however, Serra acted as an [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|NPC]], appearing in two levels of the game.<ref name="ES" /> Finally, in [[2010]], Serra and Renegade Squadron received a major role in both the dark side and the light side scenario campaigns of another expansion set of the ''Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game'', ''[[Threat of the Conqueror]]''. This set included another card depicting Serra.<ref name="Threat of the Conqueror LS" /><ref name="Threat of the Conqueror DS" />

Though clearly portrayed with a cybernetic replacement over his left eye in ''Renegade Squadron'',<ref name="RS" /> ''Elite Squadron'',<ref name="ES" /> and in Rebellion Developments' artwork,<ref name="Blog: Col Serra" /> Serra is depicted with a simple patch on both of his ''Trading Card Game'' cards.<ref name="Agents card" />

===Development===
{{Quote|Just like the story itself, the story's narrator Col Serra went through a number of iterations before he settled into his final form.|Mike Rosser, Lead Designer, Rebellion Developments|<ref name="Blog: Creating a new character" />}}
[[File:Serra_development.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The changes that Col Serra's appearance went through before the final design on the right was settled upon]]
In ''Renegade Squadron'', if the player does not pick a name for their profile, the name "Col Serra" will be assigned. When asked about this fact in the game's official development blog, the blog master Chris Baker replied that it can be interpreted that whatever character you play as in the game is Col Serra.<ref name="Blog: Q&A">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ign.com/blogs/renegadesquadron/2007/09/07/the-biweekly-qa-from-hero-animations-to-col-serras-eyepatch|text=The Biweekly Q&A: From Hero Animations to Col Serra's Eyepatch|date=2007-09-07|archiveurl=https://archive.is/1q9aH}}</ref> This, however, contradicts the fact that the player's character receives orders from Serra throughout the whole game.<ref name="RS" />

During the planning stages for ''Renegade Squadron'', the first designs of Serra depicted him as a burly [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] smuggler. The design was dropped because the producers thought that this design demonstrated obvious similarities in style with the movie [[Wikipedia:Blade Runner|''Blade Runner'']], and because they believed that people would relate better to a Human character, rather than a Zabrak. The Human character also went through two more changes in appearance before the final look was decided.<ref name="Blog: Creating a new character" /> According to an early story outline, the name of Col Serra's squadron was originally Phantom Squadron, but this was changed to Renegade Squadron. Serra was also at first intended to be questioned by a brand new [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] named [[Hes Jor-Sharu]], not Tionne.<ref name="Blog: Storyboarding the Cutscenes">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ign.com/blogs/renegadesquadron/2007/09/19/storyboarding-the-cutscenes|text=Storyboarding the Cutscenes|date=2007-09-07|archiveurl=https://archive.is/OCtX1}}</ref>
